rulings.Case Title: DOMENIC J CANNIZZARO et al VS. FRED P RICHARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel Defts To Attend And Testify At Deposition And Produce For Inspected Documents Described In Depositionnotice And For Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions Against Said Defts And His Attorneys
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 29, 2011, line 5. CROSS COMPLAINANT ANN GERVASI'S Motion For Order To Compel Defendants To Attend And Testify At Deposition And Produce For Inspected Documents Described In Deposition notice And For Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions Against Said Defendants And His Attorneys. Cross complainant's motion to compel deposition and production of documents is denied. Cross-complainants counsel should abide by the terms of the stipulation. However, even if the stipulation is not enforceable, cross-defendant had already indicated a willingness to reschedule the deposition and to turn over the requested documents thus rendering this motion unnecessary. Cross-complainant's counsel is ordered to pay cross-defendant's counsel $1500 in sanctions by July 30, 2011. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERTO A. VELASQUEZ VS. MISSION LANGUAGE AND VOCATIONAL SCHOOL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Statutory Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 29, 2011, line 5. DEFENDANTS MISSION LANGUAGE AND VOCATIONAL SCHOOL, RHINA GRANILLO, RICARDO ALVA'S Motion For Statutory Attorney's Fees. Defendant Alva's motion for statutory attorney's fees is granted. Plaintiff admits that individual employees, including supervisors, cannot be liable for discrimination claims under FEHA and Title VII. Despite this, plaintiff sued his supervisor in his individual capacity and did not dismiss him prior to the judgment on the pleadings motion filed by defendant. Therefore, the fees in the amount of $2150. are reasonable and warranted. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CHI KIN HUI VS. FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 29, 2011, line 7. DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. In looking at the terms of the policy, the allegations of the 3rd party's cross-complaint and all facts known to defendant from any source to determine coverage, the court finds that defendant has maintained its summary judgment burden. The documents indicate there was no "occurance" and the cross-complaint is based on plaintiff's intentional acts. Plaintiff's reliance on Allstate and Gray is misplaced. They are not applicable to this case. "Our Supreme Court anticipating imaginative counsel and the likelihood of artful drafting, has indicated that a third party is not the arbiter of the policy's coverage. A corollary to this rule is that the insured may not speculate about unpled third party claims to manufacture coverage." Hurley Construction Co. v. State Farm Fire and Casualty Co., (1992) 10 Cal.App. 4th 533, 538. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, A LIMITED VS. DANIEL L MITCHELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel: 1) Further Responses To Deft'S Special Interrogatories [Set One]; 2) Monetary Sanctions In The Sum Of $3040.00
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 29, 2011, line 3. DEFENDANT DANIEL MITCHELL'S Motion To Compel: 1) Further Responses To Defendant's Special Interrogatories [Set One]; 2) Monetary Sanctions In The Sum Of $3040.00. Defendant's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories (set one) is granted. If plaintiff has attorney-client privilege objections, it must provide a privilege log on the item(s). Defendant's request for sanctions in the amount of $3040.00 is granted. Plaintiff must remit the sanction amount to defendant by July 30, 2011. =(302/LMG)
